title: Timetracking by month via stacked bar chart

posts.author: NattJoy (Natt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I want to be able to create a stacked graph as seen here of time tracked per month by business (which is a status column) .

We service a lot of businesses and need to ensure we are spending the right amount of hours per month for each of those businesses. We do use regular time tracking widgets per business on another dashboard, but want this as a holistic view.

However the only way I could see to get this graph per month is by using the due date, so if the task is due in July, any (and all) hours tracked for that task, only show up under July.

Any ideas would be super helpful

Thanks!

image1441×556 82.7 KB

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml: @NattJoy if an add-on is an option, I suggest taking a look at the Analytics & Reports by Screenful. With it, you can create stacked bar charts using the time tracking data and place the charts on Monday dashboard. You can learn more from this guide.

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: My team actually created a custom coded solution in this domain. It’s a board view that allows you to choose time tracking columns on multiple boards to pull excel reports from where the time is grouped by person/board. Maybe it’ll help some teams out. It’s a private app and just a prototype for now but you can install it with this link. Let me know if you find it useful.
